mnemonics: SAME NAME, DIFFERENT SIGNATURE: Methods share a name but have unique parameter lists to be distinguished.
keyPoints: Overloaded methods must have the same name but different parameter lists (type, number, or order).
Return type alone is not sufficient to differentiate overloaded methods.
Overloading is determined at compile-time based on the reference type and arguments passed.
commonMistakes: Trying to overload a method by only changing its return type, which causes a compile-time error.
Confusing method overloading (compile-time polymorphism) with method overriding (runtime polymorphism).
